attrs.alt: Portfolio rebalancing trigger detection and response flow

content.text: Portfolio rebalancing is the deliberate reallocation of investment, team capacity, or API resources across active Claude initiatives in response to new information. Unlike stage gates — which are scheduled checkpoints — rebalancing is
: it fires when a trigger signal exceeds a threshold, not on a calendar.
A portfolio that only rebalances at quarterly reviews will spend three months over-investing in initiatives that should have been redirected in week four. Trigger-driven rebalancing cuts that waste.
Competitor ships equivalent feature; regulatory guidance issued; industry standard changes
Anthropic releases new model; pricing changes; rate limit policy update; deprecation notice
Initiative RED for 2+ sprints; eval pass rate declining; cost/query 2× budget; benefits baseline missed
Business unit de-prioritises outcome; executive sponsor changes; M&A activity; product pivot
Key engineer leaves; API quota reduced; team reorganisation; budget cut

Manually scanning for rebalancing signals across a portfolio of 10+ initiatives is impractical. A weekly automated scan consolidates initiative metrics, external signals, and platform news, then asks Claude to classify which signals cross the rebalancing threshold.

# portfolio_scan.py — weekly trigger detection across all active initiatives
import anthropic
from dataclasses import dataclass
from enum import Enum

class Severity(Enum):
    LOW      = "Low"
    MEDIUM   = "Medium"
    HIGH     = "High"
    CRITICAL = "Critical"

@dataclass
class InitiativeMetrics:
    name: str
    rag_status: str          # "Green" | "Amber" | "Red"
    rag_trend: str           # "improving" | "stable" | "worsening"
    cost_per_query_vs_budget: float   # 1.0 = on budget; 2.0 = 2x over
    eval_pass_rate: float    # 0.0 – 1.0
    eval_trend: str          # "improving" | "stable" | "declining"
    budget_consumed_pct: float  # 0.0 – 1.0 of PI budget consumed

@dataclass
class ExternalSignal:
    source: str
    description: str

def detect_triggers(
    initiatives: list[InitiativeMetrics],
    external_signals: list[ExternalSignal],
) -> str:
    client = anthropic.Anthropic()

    init_text = "\n\n".join(
        f"Initiative: {m.name}\n"
        f"RAG: {m.rag_status} ({m.rag_trend})\n"
        f"Cost/query vs budget: {m.cost_per_query_vs_budget:.1f}x\n"
        f"Eval pass rate: {m.eval_pass_rate:.0%} ({m.eval_trend})\n"
        f"PI budget consumed: {m.budget_consumed_pct:.0%}"
        for m in initiatives
    )
    ext_text = "\n".join(f"- [{s.source}] {s.description}" for s in external_signals)

    prompt = f"""You are a portfolio manager scanning a Claude AI initiative portfolio for rebalancing triggers.

INITIATIVE METRICS:
{init_text}

EXTERNAL SIGNALS THIS WEEK:
{ext_text}

For each initiative, identify any rebalancing triggers and classify severity:
- LOW: log and monitor; address at next quarterly review
- MEDIUM: RTE analysis required; within-PI adjustment possible
- HIGH: portfolio board review needed; emergency gate possible
- CRITICAL: immediate executive escalation; fund re-route or kill

Then produce:
1. TRIGGER SUMMARY — table: initiative | trigger | severity | recommended action
2. CRITICAL ACTIONS — any CRITICAL items and what must happen this week
3. MONITORING NOTES — LOW items to watch next week

Be specific. No initiative with no triggers needs to appear in the report.
    """

    response = client.messages.create(
        model="claude-haiku-4-5-20251001",
        max_tokens=900,
        messages=[{"role": "user", "content": prompt}]
    )
    return response.content[0].text

# Weekly scan
initiatives = [
    InitiativeMetrics("AI Onboarding Wizard", "Amber", "worsening", 1.1, 0.88, "declining", 0.62),
    InitiativeMetrics("Ticket Deflection",    "Green", "stable",    0.9, 0.96, "stable",    0.55),
    InitiativeMetrics("Knowledge Search",     "Red",   "worsening", 2.3, 0.79, "declining", 0.78),
    InitiativeMetrics("Mobile AI Assistant",  "Green", "improving", 1.0, 0.93, "improving", 0.40),
]
externals = [
    ExternalSignal("Anthropic blog", "claude-haiku-4-5 pricing reduced 30% effective next month"),
    ExternalSignal("Competitor news", "Rival launches AI onboarding product in same market segment"),
]
report = detect_triggers(initiatives, externals)
print(report)
Trigger detection works best when thresholds are defined upfront rather than judged case-by-case. The following thresholds are starting points; calibrate them to your organisation's risk tolerance.

# thresholds.py — configurable rebalancing trigger thresholds
from dataclasses import dataclass

@dataclass
class RebalancingThresholds:
    # RAG: consecutive sprints at status to trigger escalation
    amber_sprints_for_investigate: int = 2
    red_sprints_for_escalate: int = 1
    red_sprints_for_critical: int = 2

    # Cost per query multiplier vs. approved budget
    cpq_watch_floor: float = 1.3
    cpq_amber_floor: float = 1.8
    cpq_critical_floor: float = 2.5

    # Evaluation pass rate
    eval_amber_floor: float = 0.85
    eval_red_floor: float = 0.80
    eval_critical_floor: float = 0.75

    # PI budget consumed at midpoint (sprint 3 of 5)
    budget_amber_pct: float = 0.75
    budget_critical_pct: float = 0.90

DEFAULT_THRESHOLDS = RebalancingThresholds()

def classify_initiative(
    rag_history: list[str],        # ["Green", "Amber", "Amber"]
    cpq_ratio: float,
    eval_pass_rate: float,
    budget_consumed: float,
    is_midpoint: bool,
    thresholds: RebalancingThresholds = DEFAULT_THRESHOLDS,
) -> str:
    reasons = []

    # RAG analysis
    recent_amber = sum(1 for r in rag_history[-thresholds.amber_sprints_for_investigate:] if r == "Amber")
    recent_red   = sum(1 for r in rag_history[-thresholds.red_sprints_for_critical:]      if r == "Red")
    if recent_red >= thresholds.red_sprints_for_critical:
        reasons.append(("CRITICAL", f"Red RAG for {recent_red} consecutive sprints"))
    elif recent_red >= thresholds.red_sprints_for_escalate:
        reasons.append(("HIGH", "Red RAG status"))
    elif recent_amber >= thresholds.amber_sprints_for_investigate:
        reasons.append(("MEDIUM", f"Amber RAG for {recent_amber} consecutive sprints"))

    # Cost analysis
    if cpq_ratio >= thresholds.cpq_critical_floor:
        reasons.append(("CRITICAL", f"Cost/query {cpq_ratio:.1f}x budget"))
    elif cpq_ratio >= thresholds.cpq_amber_floor:
        reasons.append(("HIGH", f"Cost/query {cpq_ratio:.1f}x budget"))
    elif cpq_ratio >= thresholds.cpq_watch_floor:
        reasons.append(("MEDIUM", f"Cost/query {cpq_ratio:.1f}x budget — watch"))

    # Eval analysis
    if eval_pass_rate < thresholds.eval_critical_floor:
        reasons.append(("CRITICAL", f"Eval pass rate {eval_pass_rate:.0%}"))
    elif eval_pass_rate < thresholds.eval_red_floor:
        reasons.append(("HIGH", f"Eval pass rate {eval_pass_rate:.0%}"))
    elif eval_pass_rate < thresholds.eval_amber_floor:
        reasons.append(("MEDIUM", f"Eval pass rate {eval_pass_rate:.0%}"))

    if not reasons:
        return "GREEN — no rebalancing triggers"

    severity_rank = {"CRITICAL": 3, "HIGH": 2, "MEDIUM": 1}
    worst = max(reasons, key=lambda r: severity_rank[r[0]])
    detail = "; ".join(f"{s}: {r}" for s, r in reasons)
    return f"{worst[0]} — {detail}"

# Knowledge Search example
print(classify_initiative(
    rag_history=["Green", "Amber", "Red"],
    cpq_ratio=2.3,
    eval_pass_rate=0.79,
    budget_consumed=0.78,
    is_midpoint=True,
))
Once a trigger is confirmed, the portfolio board chooses one of five rebalancing actions. Claude can model the trade-offs for each option before the board convenes.
Strong early signals; competitor pressure; strategic priority
On track; signals are noise; mid-PI — too early to act
Cost overrun; team capacity insufficient; lower-priority features
Free up capacity for higher-ROI work
Core hypothesis wrong but adjacent opportunity identified
Pivot team to new problem statement
Sustained underperformance; no path to ROI; regulatory blocker

# rebalancing_options.py — model trade-offs for each rebalancing action
import anthropic
from dataclasses import dataclass

@dataclass
class RebalancingContext:
    initiative_name: str
    trigger_summary: str
    investment_to_date: str
    remaining_pi_budget: str
    team_size: int
    strategic_priority: str    # "critical" | "high" | "medium" | "low"
    exit_options: list[str]    # what could the freed capacity go to

def generate_rebalancing_options(ctx: RebalancingContext) -> str:
    client = anthropic.Anthropic()
    prompt = f"""Generate a rebalancing options analysis for a portfolio investment committee.

Initiative: {ctx.initiative_name}
Trigger: {ctx.trigger_summary}
Investment to date: {ctx.investment_to_date}
Remaining PI budget: {ctx.remaining_pi_budget}
Team size: {ctx.team_size} engineers
Strategic priority: {ctx.strategic_priority}
If freed, capacity could go to: {", ".join(ctx.exit_options)}

For each of these 5 options — Increase / Maintain / Reduce Scope / Redirect / Kill —
provide a 2-3 bullet analysis of: conditions where this option is correct, what it
costs, and its primary risk. End with a RECOMMENDATION and one-sentence rationale.

Be direct. No option that is clearly inapplicable needs more than one sentence.
    """
    response = client.messages.create(
        model="claude-haiku-4-5-20251001",
        max_tokens=900,
        messages=[{"role": "user", "content": prompt}]
    )
    return response.content[0].text

ctx = RebalancingContext(
    initiative_name="Knowledge Search",
    trigger_summary="CRITICAL: Red RAG 2 sprints; cost/query 2.3x budget; eval pass rate 79% declining",
    investment_to_date="$320k (2.5 PIs)",
    remaining_pi_budget="$80k",
    team_size=4,
    strategic_priority="medium",
    exit_options=["AI Onboarding Wizard (higher ROI)", "New initiative: AI content moderation (Gate 0 approved)"],
)
print(generate_rebalancing_options(ctx))
Platform-Driven Triggers — Responding to Anthropic Releases
Model platform changes are unique triggers because they affect

. A new Claude model, a pricing change, or a deprecation notice can shift the cost-benefit calculation for the entire portfolio overnight. The portfolio manager needs a rapid triage process to assess portfolio-wide impact before individual teams act unilaterally.

# model_release_triage.py — assess portfolio impact of a new Claude model release
import anthropic
from dataclasses import dataclass

@dataclass
class InitiativeProfile:
    name: str
    current_model: str
    use_case: str           # brief description
    latency_sensitive: bool
    reasoning_heavy: bool
    monthly_token_volume: int
    cost_per_query_usd: float

@dataclass
class ModelRelease:
    new_model_id: str
    summary: str            # release notes summary
    price_change_pct: float # vs. comparable tier; negative = cheaper
    latency_change_pct: float
    capability_highlights: list[str]
    deprecated_models: list[str]

def assess_release_impact(
    initiatives: list[InitiativeProfile],
    release: ModelRelease,
) -> str:
    client = anthropic.Anthropic()

    init_text = "\n".join(
        f"- {i.name}: model={i.current_model}, latency_sensitive={i.latency_sensitive}, "
        f"reasoning_heavy={i.reasoning_heavy}, monthly_tokens={i.monthly_token_volume:,}, "
        f"cpq=${i.cost_per_query_usd:.4f}, use_case={i.use_case}"
        for i in initiatives
    )

    prompt = f"""A new Claude model has been released. Assess the portfolio-wide impact.

NEW MODEL: {release.new_model_id}
Summary: {release.summary}
Price change: {release.price_change_pct:+.0f}% vs. comparable tier
Latency change: {release.latency_change_pct:+.0f}%
Capabilities: {", ".join(release.capability_highlights)}
Deprecated models: {", ".join(release.deprecated_models) or "none"}

ACTIVE INITIATIVES:
{init_text}

For each initiative, provide:
1. IMPACT: High / Medium / Low / None
2. RECOMMENDED ACTION: Upgrade now / Evaluate / No change / Required upgrade (if deprecated)
3. ESTIMATED COST DELTA: monthly cost change if they upgrade

Then: PORTFOLIO PRIORITY ORDER for upgrades (which initiative benefits most, do first)
    """

    response = client.messages.create(
        model="claude-haiku-4-5-20251001",
        max_tokens=900,
        messages=[{"role": "user", "content": prompt}]
    )
    return response.content[0].text

# Example: haiku pricing cut + new model
release = ModelRelease(
    new_model_id="claude-haiku-4-5-20251001-v2",
    summary="30% price reduction; 20% latency improvement; better instruction following",
    price_change_pct=-30,
    latency_change_pct=-20,
    capability_highlights=["improved instruction following", "longer context at same price"],
    deprecated_models=[],
)
initiatives = [
    InitiativeProfile("AI Onboarding Wizard", "claude-haiku-4-5-20251001", "step-by-step user guidance",
                      latency_sensitive=True, reasoning_heavy=False, monthly_token_volume=2_000_000, cost_per_query_usd=0.0018),
    InitiativeProfile("Knowledge Search", "claude-haiku-4-5-20251001", "document retrieval and ranking",
                      latency_sensitive=True, reasoning_heavy=False, monthly_token_volume=4_000_000, cost_per_query_usd=0.0023),
    InitiativeProfile("Ticket Deflection", "claude-haiku-4-5-20251001", "intent classification + resolution",
                      latency_sensitive=False, reasoning_heavy=True, monthly_token_volume=1_500_000, cost_per_query_usd=0.0012),
]
print(assess_release_impact(initiatives, release))
Clear decision rights prevent rebalancing from stalling in committee or being resolved unilaterally by teams. The following RACI covers the four decision types.
Slow governance is itself a portfolio risk. Define maximum time-to-decision for each severity level and track actual cycle times.
Escalate to HIGH if unresolved at Sprint 4
Board notification; public disclosure if material

# decision_latency.py — track governance cycle time against SLA
from dataclasses import dataclass
from datetime import date, timedelta

@dataclass
class RebalancingDecision:
    initiative_name: str
    trigger_detected: date
    severity: str
    decision_made: date | None
    decision: str | None

SLA_DAYS = {"LOW": 90, "MEDIUM": 70, "HIGH": 5, "CRITICAL": 2}

def audit_decision_latency(decisions: list[RebalancingDecision], today: date) -> list[dict]:
    results = []
    for d in decisions:
        sla = SLA_DAYS.get(d.severity, 90)
        deadline = d.trigger_detected + timedelta(days=sla)
        if d.decision_made:
            cycle = (d.decision_made - d.trigger_detected).days
            status = "ON TIME" if d.decision_made <= deadline else "BREACHED"
        else:
            cycle = (today - d.trigger_detected).days
            status = "PENDING" if today <= deadline else "OVERDUE"
        results.append({
            "initiative": d.initiative_name,
            "severity": d.severity,
            "cycle_days": cycle,
            "sla_days": sla,
            "status": status,
            "decision": d.decision or "pending",
        })
    return results

today = date(2026, 7, 2)
decisions = [
    RebalancingDecision("Knowledge Search", date(2026, 6, 10), "CRITICAL",
                        date(2026, 6, 11), "Kill — fund re-routed to AI Onboarding"),
    RebalancingDecision("AI Onboarding Wizard", date(2026, 6, 20), "MEDIUM",
                        date(2026, 7, 1), "Reduce scope — defer stretch features to next PI"),
    RebalancingDecision("Mobile AI Assistant", date(2026, 6, 28), "HIGH",
                        None, None),
]
for r in audit_decision_latency(decisions, today):
    print(f"[{r['status']}] {r['initiative']} ({r['severity']}): {r['cycle_days']}d / {r['sla_days']}d SLA — {r['decision']}")

Portfolio rebalancing triggers for Claude initiatives fall into five categories — external market, model platform, portfolio performance, strategy, and capacity — each requiring a calibrated severity classification before escalation. Claude accelerates the weekly scanning cycle by ingesting initiative metrics and external signals, classifying severity against predefined thresholds, and generating trade-off analyses for the investment committee. The discipline is not in the tools but in the thresholds: pre-agreed, written-down criteria remove the subjectivity that allows underperforming initiatives to survive on political momentum rather than evidence.
